w:r.w:t: Full MVP Specification | Hackathon Build Document | June 2026
Built for: V1TROUS Hackathon — Healthcare Track
Stack: Next.js 14 · Tailwind CSS · Groq API (free) · localStorage

CareCompanion is a web dashboard for family caregivers managing the health of an elderly parent or dependent. It combines two powerful features: an AI-powered medical record reader that explains reports in plain English, and a personal health tracker for medications, symptoms, and appointments — all in one place.
The problem: Millions of family caregivers in India have no digital tool built for them. They manage critical health decisions using WhatsApp messages and paper notes.
"40 million unpaid family caregivers in India have no digital tool built for them.
CareCompanion is the first AI-powered app designed for this exact person."
Upload any medical report (PDF or text) and get a plain-English AI summary
Chat with your uploaded report — ask follow-up questions in simple language
Track all medications with daily dose check-off
Log symptoms with severity scores and get AI pattern analysis
Manage upcoming and past doctor appointments
Dashboard home with a unified summary of everything
70B parameters — much better medical language understanding than 8B models
Groq runs it extremely fast — responses feel near-instant
Free tier is more than enough for a 2-week hackathon demo
Do NOT use llama-3.1-8b-instant (too shallow for medical text)
Do NOT use whisper models (those are audio transcription only)
│ ├── groq.ts (Groq client — SERVER ONLY, has server-only import)
This is non-negotiable. The Groq API key must NEVER reach the browser. All AI calls flow through Next.js server-side API routes only.
Data flow — how AI calls work
→ Next.js API route reads GROQ_API_KEY from process.env (server only)
The frontend never sees the API key. It only calls your own /api/* routes.
.env.local (never committed — your real key goes here)
import "server-only"; // Build fails if accidentally imported client-side
API route safety rules — apply to every file in app/api/
Add comment at top: // SERVER ONLY — do not import from client components
Validate all incoming request body fields before passing to Groq
Cap input length: const text = body.text.slice(0, 12000) — prevents abuse
Never return raw Groq errors to frontend — they may leak model/key info
Always catch errors and return a generic message: { error: 'AI request failed' }
The home screen. Gives the caregiver an instant overview of everything.
Upcoming appointments in the next 7 days
Recent activity list — last 5 actions across all modules
Quick action buttons: Upload Report, Log Symptom, Add Medication
Data source: Read counts from localStorage across all module keys.
The flagship feature. Upload a medical report and have a conversation with AI about it.
Left panel (40% width) — file upload and past reports
Right panel (60% width) — AI chat interface
On upload: extract text, auto-generate AI summary, display below filename
List of previously uploaded reports stored in localStorage
Click a past report to reload it into chat context
Message list with user bubbles (right) and AI bubbles (left)
Input box fixed at bottom with send button
When no report loaded: show 'Upload a report to start chatting'
Stream AI tokens to the UI as they arrive — do not wait for full response
"You are a medical report interpreter for family caregivers with no medical
background. Explain this report in simple, clear English. Highlight anything
concerning. Avoid all jargon. Use short paragraphs."
"You are a helpful health assistant. The user is a family caregiver.
Answer questions about the medical report below in plain English.
// Output: ReadableStream — stream tokens back to the frontend
// Frontend: use fetch with a reader, append tokens as they arrive
PDF files: use pdf-parse — read as buffer in the API route, extract .text
TXT files: read as plain UTF-8 text
Store in localStorage as: { id, name, text, summary, uploadedAt }
Track all medications the patient is taking, with daily dose check-off.
Add Medication button — opens a modal/form
Progress bar at top: 'X of Y doses taken today'
List of medication cards — one per medication
Select: Once daily / Twice daily / Three times / As needed
Today's check-off buttons — one per dose time (Morning ✓ / Evening ✗)
Log daily health observations for the patient with severity tracking.
Log Symptom button — opens a form
Filter bar: All / This week / This month
AI Analysis button — sends last 30 symptoms to Groq, shows patterns
List of symptom entries in reverse chronological order
Severity badge — color coded: 1-2 green, 3 yellow, 4-5 red
Send last 30 symptom entries as JSON context
Prompt: 'You are a health analyst. Review these symptom logs for a patient. Identify any patterns, recurring issues, or things a caregiver should bring up with a doctor.'
Display result in an expandable panel below the filter bar
Track upcoming and past doctor visits.
Add Appointment button — opens a form
Two sections: Upcoming (soonest first) and Past
Each card: doctor name, specialty, date/time, location, status badge
Completed appointments: Add Notes button for post-visit summary
App name CareCompanion with heart icon at top
Navigation links: Dashboard, Records, Medications, Symptoms, Appointments
Active link highlighted with teal background
5 icon+label tabs for the same 5 pages
On /records page: show currently loaded report filename
Card style: white background, subtle shadow, rounded-xl (border-radius: 12px)
All forms use controlled React inputs
Toast notifications for all save / delete / error actions — use react-hot-toast
Loading spinner on all AI calls — never show a blank state while waiting
Responsive: sidebar on desktop, bottom nav on mobile
npm install groq-sdk pdf-parse react-hot-toast server-only
Create .env.local in the project root (this file is gitignored):
Get your free Groq API key
Sign up for free — no credit card needed
Add GROQ_API_KEY as an environment variable in the Vercel dashboard under Project Settings → Environment Variables.
Init Next.js, install deps, set up Tailwind, create sidebar + layout, .gitignore, .env.local
File upload component, pdf-parse integration, /api/summarize route with Groq, display summary
/api/chat streaming route, ChatWindow component, stream tokens to UI, connect to uploaded report context
Add/edit/delete meds, daily dose check-off, progress bar, localStorage persistence
Symptom log with severity, AI analysis button, appointment tracker with upcoming/past split
Home summary cards, recent activity feed, responsive mobile nav, loading states, toasts
Deploy to Vercel, set env vars, test end-to-end, prepare demo flow and pitch story
Build order tip: Start with /records (upload + AI chat) first.
It is the most impressive feature and proves the AI integration works.
Everything else is standard CRUD — do that after the AI is solid.
10. MVP Scope — What to skip for now
Do NOT build in this 2 weeks
Image or scan uploads (only text-based PDFs for now)
These are great v2 features to mention in your demo pitch.
Judges love when you show you know what to cut and what to keep.
Walk judges through this exact flow — it tells a story:
"My grandmother's cardiologist gave us a 4-page report. My parents had no idea what it meant."
Drag a real-looking PDF onto the Records page. Watch it parse instantly.
Point to the plain-English summary. "No jargon. Any caregiver can understand this."
Type: 'Should I be worried about the cholesterol value?' Show the AI responding.
Switch to Symptoms. Log 'chest tightness' severity 4. Show the red badge.
Show the medication page with today's dose check-off. 'She hasn't taken her evening dose yet.'
Hit the AI Analysis button on Symptoms. Show the pattern detection output.
"One app. Real AI. Built for 40 million people who need it."
